title: fraxinus angustifolia subsp. persica
intro: Fraxinus angustifolia subsp. persica, also known as the English or Persian manna ash, is a subspecies of the Fraxinus angustifolia tree native to the Mediterranean region. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus angustifolia subsp. persica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
